data.title: APX Lending Secures $20M Funding Amid 'Rising Demand' for Crypto-Backed Loans in Canada
data.description:
• Crypto-backed loan provider APX Lending has secured a $20 million accordion facility from Cypress Hills, a private credit investment firm.
  • An accordion facility is a provision that allows a borrower to increase their borrowing without renegotiating the entire agreement.
  • Digital asset lending has a somewhat chequered history, with various lenders collapsing in 2022 at the onset of the crypto winter, although that might be changing with the evolving U.S. regulatory regime.

data.title: SOL, DOGE Lead Gains Among Majors as Analyst Eye Next Big Move Higher
data.description:
• Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies continue to zig and zag, rallying on Friday after plunging Thursday folllowing Wednesday's big gains..
  • Solana and dogecoin led gains among major cryptocurrencies, while ether underperformed.
  • Analysts suggest that the market's positive reaction could lead to further gains, with bitcoin potentially reaching $100,000 by year-end.

data.title: Crypto Rebound Likely as Trump Tariffs May Bring Down Inflation
data.description:
• Despite fears of stagflation, market indicators suggest Trump's tariffs may lead to lower inflation in the long term, potentially allowing the Federal Reserve to cut rates.
  • Historical and recent analysis indicate that tariffs tend to be disinflationary in advanced economies as they can lead to reduced consumer spending and lower prices.

data.title: Dollar Index Falls to Lowest Level in 3 Years, While BTC Remains Steady
data.description:
• The DXY Index has dropped below levels seen at the same point during Trump’s first term.
  • Market participants continue to move away from U.S. assets, causing the U.S. dollar to weaken amid escalating trade tensions with China.
  • China has increased tariffs on U.S. goods to a cumulative 125%, intensifying the trade war.

data.title: Altcoin Action in Powertrade's Options Market Heats Up Driven by XRP, SOL and DOGE
data.description:
• PowerTrade's options market for altcoins saw increased activity due to market volatility, with traders seeking hedging and speculative opportunities.
  • XRP options trading volume exceeded $5 million, with a balance between bullish and bearish positions, indicating expectations of significant short-term price movements.
  • Despite macroeconomic concerns, traders showed upside interest DOGE while seeking protective plays in SOL.

data.title: Bitcoin’s Recent Drawdown Proves Its More Than Just a Leveraged Tech Play
data.description:
• Bitcoin’s current 3-month drawdown is significantly less severe than during the 2021-2022 correction, reflecting improved market stability.
  • Among the "Magnificent Seven," bitcoin's performance ranks mid-pack, outperforming Tesla and NVIDIA, and matching declines seen in Apple, Meta, and Amazon.

data.title: World Liberty Says it Hasn't Sold Any Ether, Refutes Arkham Data
data.description:
• The project denies selling ETH or any of its other positions.
  • It remains at a nine-figure loss after acquiring several assets over the past six months.

data.title: CryptoPunk NFT, Once Bought for $16M, Sold for $10M Loss
data.description:
• CryptoPunks, once a symbol of NFT prestige, saw a record sale of $56 million in 2024, but recent sales indicate a market downturn.
  • The third-highest NFT sale, CryptoPunk #3100, was recently sold for 4,000 ETH, marking a significant loss from its previous value.
  • Despite declining NFT trading volumes, rare CryptoPunks like #3100 still command high prices due to their scarcity and unique features.

data.title: Gold Rally Makes Tether’s XAUT Top-Performing Digital Asset as Crypto Markets Remain Flat
data.description:
• Tokenized gold remains popular among crypto investors in Asia, with Tether's XAUT showing strong market performance.
  • Despite a de-escalation in trade tensions, investors are drawn to gold due to concerns over unpredictable U.S. policy and a growing budget deficit.

data.title: S&P 500 More Volatile Than Bitcoin as U.S. Assets Lose Investor Favor
data.description:
• Bitcoin's volatility has decreased compared to the S&P 500, evolving as a low-beta play against stocks.
  • Investors are moving away from U.S. assets, causing a rise in Treasury yields and a decline in the dollar index and U.S. stocks.
